rubrics.name: Evaluation Quick Practice Reset Deadline Rubric

criteria.title: Acknowledge The Deadline Pressure
criteria.description: Check whether the response explicitly recognizes why the date matters.

levels.description: Fail: the response does not acknowledge why the date matters.
examples: Moves straight into logistics without recognizing the pressure.

levels.description: Pass: the response directly acknowledges why the date matters.
examples: Names the pressure or importance behind the request.

criteria.title: Set A Realistic Boundary
criteria.description: Check whether the response sets a realistic next step, timing, or boundary.

levels.description: Fail: the response stays vague or makes an unrealistic promise.
examples: Says the team will try without giving a realistic boundary.

levels.description: Pass: the response sets a realistic next step, timing, or boundary.
examples: Gives a realistic date or checkpoint instead of an empty reassurance.

rubrics.name: Evaluation Quick Practice Scope Tradeoff Rubric

criteria.title: Reflect The Sponsor Goal
criteria.description: Check whether the response reflects what the sponsor is trying to achieve.

levels.description: Fail: the response does not reflect the sponsor goal.
examples: Proposes a tradeoff without acknowledging the sponsor priority.

levels.description: Pass: the response reflects the sponsor goal before giving the tradeoff.
examples: Names the goal or urgency that matters to the sponsor.

criteria.title: Offer A Concrete Tradeoff
criteria.description: Check whether the response offers a concrete tradeoff instead of vague reassurance.

levels.description: Fail: the response avoids a concrete tradeoff.
examples: Promises progress soon without specifying scope or timing.

levels.description: Pass: the response offers a concrete tradeoff with a realistic follow-up.
examples: Suggests a narrowed scope and a concrete checkpoint.

cases.label: Reset an impossible deadline with empathy and a realistic next step

prompt.title: Reset the timeline
prompt.prompt_text: A client asks for an impossible delivery date. Respond with empathy and a realistic next step.

cases.response_text: I hear why the date matters to you. The earliest realistic delivery date is next Friday, and I can confirm any scope tradeoffs with the team by tomorrow afternoon.

cases.label: Weak response that reassures without a usable boundary

prompt.title: Vague reassurance
prompt.prompt_text: A client pushes for a date you cannot commit to. Respond in a way that manages expectations.

cases.response_text: I know this is important and I will do my best to make it happen. We should have something soon and I will keep you posted.
